data.title: Crypto Scam Victim Files Lawsuit Against Two Banks Over Alleged Negligence
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Michael Zidell, who claims to be a victim of a crypto romance scam, has filed a lawsuit against East West Bank and Cathay Bank in a California federal court. Zidell accuses the banks of neglecting their statutory duties and obligations by failing to identify suspicious transactions. He alleges that he transferred nearly $7 million to an account at East West Bank and over $9.7 million to an account at Cathay Bank, both allegedly controlled by scammers.

Zidell's legal actions follow a previous lawsuit against Citibank, filed on June 24, where he accused the bank of ignoring red flags in 12 transfers totaling approximately $4 million. The lawsuits stem from a relationship Zidell claims to have formed with "Carolyn Parker," a supposed business owner he met on Facebook in early 2023. Parker allegedly convinced Zidell to invest in non-fungible tokens (NFTs), leading him to transfer over $20 million to various bank accounts provided by a trading platform she recommended. Zidell claims the platform disappeared by April 2023, along with his investments.

The lawsuit against East West Bank and Cathay Bank seeks to hold them accountable for negligence and for allegedly aiding and abetting securities fraud. Zidell argues that the banks facilitated the scam by providing services such as opening accounts and processing wire transfers without adequately monitoring for suspicious activity. He contends that the banks should have investigated the large, round-number transactions that were indicative of fraudulent activity.

Additionally, Zidell's complaint includes allegations of "aiding and abetting elder abuse" against East West Bank and Cathay Bank, a charge not made against Citibank. While Zidell's age is not specified in the complaint, California law defines an elder as someone 65 years or older. Zidell is seeking compensatory damages, legal costs, and interest through a jury trial.

data.title: Transfer Alert: Sui and Aptos Address Compatibility Issues
data.description: According to PANews, SlowMist's Cosine has issued a warning regarding the incompatibility between Sui and Aptos addresses. Users are advised to exercise caution when transferring funds, as the private key for a Sui address cannot derive the same address for Aptos, making conversions between the two impossible. Cosine explained that if a user mistakenly transfers Aptos funds to a Sui address, the funds will be irretrievable. Despite both addresses appearing valid with a '0x' prefix and consisting of 66 characters, they are fundamentally different. Users should ensure they are transferring to the correct address type to avoid loss.

data.title: SlowMist Cosine: The private key corresponding to the Sui address cannot derive the Aptos address of the same address, and the two cannot be converted to each other
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News SlowMist Yuxian issued a reminder on the X platform that users should pay attention to the private key corresponding to the Sui address when transferring money. It cannot derive the Aptos address of the same address, and the two cannot be converted to each other. "For example, if you have Aptos and accidentally transfer to your Sui address, then the funds are gone. We studied it and found that we can't find it back." "In other words, the same mnemonic, derived according to the logic of Aptos Sui, has different addresses. But when transferring money, it will be considered a legal address. (Starting with 0x, a total of 66 characters)."

data.title: Binance Alpha will launch CROSS, with a threshold of 140 points for airdrops
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Binance Alpha will launch CROSS (CROSS), and trading will start at 16:00 (UTC+8) on July 4, 2025.
Eligible users can use Binance Alpha points to claim 500 CROSS tokens on the event page within 24 hours after trading starts. Users with at least 140 Binance Alpha points can participate in the airdrop on a first-come, first-served basis until the airdrop pool is fully distributed or the airdrop event ends.

data.title: Bitget Onchain launches W, M, and economy tokens
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget Onchain Trading (Onchain) has launched Solana and BNB Smart Chain's MEME tokens W, M, and economy. Users can start trading in the on-chain trading section.
Bitget Onchain aims to seamlessly connect CEX and DEX to provide users with a more convenient, efficient, and secure on-chain trading experience. Users can directly use Bitget spot accounts (USDT/USDC) to trade popular assets on the chain. Currently, popular public chains such as Solana (SOL), BNB Smart Chain (BSC) and Base are supported.

data.title: Bitget Wallet natively connects to Katana mainnet, supporting users to participate in 1 billion KAT mining incentive plan
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Web3 wallet Bitget Wallet has been connected to the Katana mainnet built for DeFi scenarios. Users can add the Katana mainnet through Bitget Wallet to perform basic operations such as transfer and receipt, and connect to Katana DApp to interact and earn KAT income.
Katana is a Layer2 network jointly incubated by Polygon and GSR. It is designed for efficient asset matching and composable DeFi modules, aiming to bring users a better on-chain transaction and lending experience. Currently, Katana has officially launched a native token incentive plan with a total of 1 billion KAT to encourage users to participate in early liquidity mining.

data.title: Bitcoin's early whale transferred 10,000 BTC after 14 years of silence
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, an old Bitcoin wallet that has been silent for more than 14 years has just transferred out 10,000 BTC, which is about 1.09 billion US dollars at the current price. The wallet initially purchased Bitcoin at a price of only 0.78 US dollars. This transfer is equivalent to a return of about 140,000 times.

data.title: Trump plans to impose tariffs of up to 70% on some countries, effective August 1
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the latest public statement, US President Trump plans to impose tariffs of varying proportions on different countries, ranging from 10%-20% to 60%-70%.
